entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
The discussion highlights the potential for increasing the utility of main chain transactions as a strategy to encourage higher fee contributions. It is noted that certain types of transactions, such as those involving the opening and closing of lightning channels, can be more valuable due to their aggregated nature compared to simple money transfers. The increase in transaction utility is associated with a corresponding rise in transaction value.
The argument further observes that there is significant scope for elevating fee levels through enhanced transaction utility, a phenomenon already witnessed in various other networks. These networks have experienced rapid increases in fees following enhancements in transaction utility. Drawing on these precedents, the suggestion is made that planned, gradual improvements in the utility of on-chain transactions could effectively compensate for any reduction in rewards, thereby maintaining adequate security levels for the network. This approach underlines a proactive strategy to adapt to changes in network dynamics by enhancing the intrinsic value of transactions conducted on the chain.
